volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 1293 - Section 1487)

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: ἄπιστον εἶπας μῦθον · ὃν δʼ ἰδεῖν θέλεις
sections.translation: What you say is incredible ; but the one you want to see , the lord of the country , has gone in haste from the temple .

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ποῖ ; δεῖ γὰρ αὐτὸν εἰδέναι τὰ δρώμενα .
sections.translation: Where ? For he should know what has been done .

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.original: οὐκ ἴσμεν · ἀλλὰ στεῖχε καὶ δίωκέ νιν
sections.translation: We don ’ t know ; but go after him , and report these things to him where you find him .

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: ὁρᾶτʼ , ἄπιστον ὡς γυναικεῖον γένος ·
sections.translation: See , what a faithless race you women are ! You also have a share in what has been done .

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: μαίνῃ · τί δʼ ἡμῖν τῶν ξένων δρασμοῦ μέτα ;
sections.translation: You are mad ! What do we have to do the the flight of the strangers ? Go as quickly as you can to the ruler ’ s door !

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: οὔ , πρίν γʼ ἂν εἴπῃ τοὔπος ἑρμηνεὺς ὅδε ,
sections.translation: No ! Not until this interpreter brings word if the king is inside or not . Ho there ! Unbar the doors — I am speaking to those within —

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: καὶ δεσπότῃ σημήναθʼ οὕνεκʼ ἐν πύλαις
sections.translation: and inform the master that I am at the gate with a burden of bad news .

sections.label: Section 1307
sections.original: τίς ἀμφὶ δῶμα θεᾶς τόδʼ ἵστησιν βοήν ,
sections.translation: ( appearing at the temple door .) Who is raising this clamor at the temple of the goddess , striking at the gates and sending his noise within ?

sections.label: Section 1309
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! These women told me that you were outside ; they would have driven me away from the temple ,

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: ὡς ἐκτὸς εἴης · σὺ δὲ κατʼ οἶκον ἦσθʼ ἄρα .
sections.translation: but you were inside after all .

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: τί προσδοκῶσαι κέρδος ἢ θηρώμεναι ;
sections.translation: What advantage were they expecting and hunting after ?

sections.label: Section 1312
sections.original: αὖθις τὰ τῶνδε σημανῶ · τὰ δʼ ἐν ποσὶ
sections.translation: I will tell you about them later ; hear what is currently at hand . The girl who presided at this altar , Iphigenia , has left the country

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: σὺν τοῖς ξένοισιν οἴχεται , σεμνὸν θεᾶς
sections.translation: with the strangers , and takes with her the holy statue of the goddess ; the purification was a cheat .

sections.label: Section 1317
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; τί πνεῦμα συμφορᾶς κεκτημένη ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? What influence in her character brought her to this ?

sections.label: Section 1318
sections.original: σῴζουσʼ Ὀρέστην · τοῦτο γὰρ σὺ θαυμάσῃ .
sections.translation: To save Orestes ; here is a marvel for you !

sections.label: Section 1319
sections.original: τὸν ποῖον ; ἆρʼ ὃν Τυνδαρὶς τίκτει κόρη ;
sections.translation: To save whom ? Clytemnestra ’ s son ?

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: ὃν τοῖσδε βωμοῖς θεὰ καθωσιώσατο .
sections.translation: The one whom the goddess was dedicating to herself at this altar .

sections.label: Section 1321
sections.original: ὦ θαῦμα — πῶς σε μεῖζον ὀνομάσας τύχω ;
sections.translation: Marvelous ! for what more can it be called ?

sections.label: Section 1322
sections.original: μὴ ʼνταῦθα τρέψῃς σὴν φρένʼ , ἀλλʼ ἄκουέ μου ·
sections.translation: Do not think of that , but listen to me ; consider it clearly and when you hear , devise a pursuit to hunt down the strangers .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: λέγʼ · εὖ γὰρ εἶπας · οὐ γὰρ ἀγχίπλουν πόρον
sections.translation: Speak ; you have said well ; for their flight is not so brief a voyage as to escape my spear .

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.original: ἐπεὶ πρὸς ἀκτὰς ἤλθομεν θαλασσίας ,
sections.translation: When we came to the sea-shore , where Orestes ’ ship was moored in hiding ,

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ἔχοντας , ἐξένευσʼ ἀποστῆναι πρόσω
sections.translation: Agamemnon ’ s daughter motioned to those of us you sent with the strangers ’ bonds to stand far off , as if her sacrifice of purifying flame , that she had come for , were secret . But she went on alone , holding the strangers ’ chains in her hands , behind them . Your servants , lord , were suspicious ,

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: ἤρεσκε μέντοι σοῖσι προσπόλοις , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: but we allowed it . After a while , so that we might think that she was accomplishing something , she raised a shout , and chanted strange songs and spells , as if she were washing off the pollution of murder . When we had sat a long time ,

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ἐσῆλθεν ἡμᾶς μὴ λυθέντες οἱ ξένοι
sections.translation: it occurred to us that the strangers , loosed from their bonds , might kill her and escape by flight . But we were afraid of seeing what we ought not , and sat in silence . But at length we all resolved to go where they were , although we were not allowed .

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: κἀνταῦθʼ ὁρῶμεν Ἑλλάδος νεὼς σκάφος
sections.translation: There we saw a Hellene ship , winged with ready blade for the stroke , and at the oar-locks were fifty rowers with their oars ; the two youths stood by the stern , freed from their chains .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: κοντοῖς δὲ πρῷραν εἶχον , οἳ δʼ ἐπωτίδων
sections.translation: Some were holding the prow in place with poles ; others were fastening the anchor from the cat-heads ; others were drawing the stern-cables through their hands , and making haste to let down the ladders into the sea for the strangers . Without sparing ourselves , when we saw

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: δόλια τεχνήματʼ , εἰχόμεσθα τῆς ξένης
sections.translation: their treacherous wiles , we seized the priestess and the cables , and tried to draw the ship ’ s rudder-oars out through their holes . Then there was a debate : What is your reason for carrying the statue and the priestess away from the land by theft ?

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: τίνος τίς ὢν σὺ τήνδʼ ἀπεμπολᾷς χθονός ;
sections.translation: Who is your father , who are you , to smuggle her away ? He said : Know that I am Orestes , her brother , Agamemnon ’ s son , and I have come to take my sister , whom I lost from her home .

sections.label: Section 1364
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲν ἧσσον εἰχόμεσθα τῆς ξένης
sections.translation: But we held her no less ,

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: καὶ πρὸς σὲ ἕπεσθαι διεβιαζόμεσθά νιν ·
sections.translation: and were leading her to you by force , for which I received these dreadful blows on my cheeks ; they had no swords , nor did we . Both the youths gave rattling blows with their fists ,

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: ἐς πλευρὰ καὶ πρὸς ἧπαρ ἠκοντίζετο ,
sections.translation: darting their limbs against our sides and breasts , so that as soon as we joined battle , we were worn out . We were fleeing to the cliff , stamped with dreadful marks , some with bloody wounds on their heads , others on their eyes ;

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: ὄχθοις δʼ ἐπισταθέντες εὐλαβεστέρως
sections.translation: when we stood on the on the heights , we fought more cautiously and hurled rocks at them . But , standing on the stern , the archers with their arrows kept us off and drove us away . And now an immense swelling wave ran the ship aground ,

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: πρὸς γῆν , φόβος δʼ ἦν παρθένῳ τέγξαι πόδα —
sections.translation: and the maiden was afraid to get her feet wet . Orestes bore his sister on his left arm , going into the sea and quickly up the ladder , and he set her on the ship , along with the statue of Zeus ’ daughter , fallen from heaven .

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: ἄγαλμα . ναὸς δʼ ἐκ μέσης ἐφθέγξατο
sections.translation: From the middle of the ship , he cried out : Sailors of Hellas , seize the ship with the oars and make the waves white with foam ; for we possess those things for which we sailed the inhospitable straits , within the clashing rocks .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: οἳ δὲ στεναγμὸν ἡδὺν ἐκβρυχώμενοι
sections.translation: They gave a cheerful shout , and struck the salt wave . The ship , while it was within the harbor , was headed for the mouth ; but when it had crossed , it met with a violent swell aand was hard pressed ; and the wind , rising with sudden dreadful gusts ,

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: ὠθεῖ παλίμπρυμνʼ ἱστίʼ · οἳ δʼ ἐκαρτέρουν
sections.translation: forced it astern . They beat the waves strongly ; but the swell was driving the ship back towards the land . Agamemnon ’ s daughter stood up and prayed : O daughter of Leto , bring me , your priestess , safely to Hellas

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: ἐκ βαρβάρου γῆς καὶ κλοπαῖς σύγγνωθʼ ἐμαῖς .
sections.translation: from this barbaric land , and forgive my thefts . For you , goddess , love your brother ; believe that I love mine also . The sailors shouted the paean in response to her prayer , and applied their naked shoulders

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: κώπῃ προσαρμόσαντες ἐκ κελεύσματος .
sections.translation: to the oars , at the command . But the ship came nearer and nearer to the rocks ; some of us rushed into the sea , others grasped the woven ropes . And I set out here to you at once , lord ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: σοὶ τὰς ἐκεῖθεν σημανῶν , ἄναξ , τύχας .
sections.translation: to tell you what has happened there . But go , take chains and nets with you ; for if the swell does not become calm , there is no hope of safety for the strangers .

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: σεμνὸς Ποσειδῶν , Πελοπίδαις ἐναντίος ,
sections.translation: Revered Poseidon , ruler of the sea , watches over Troy and is hostile to the race of Pelops ; he will now allow you and your citizens , as is right , to have in your hands the son of Agamemnon and his sister ; she stands convicted as betrayer of her unremembered sacrifice to the goddess in Aulis .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον Ἰφιγένεια , συγγόνου μέτα
sections.translation: Unhappy Iphigenia , you will die with your brother , if you come again into the hands of the king .

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: ὦ πάντες ἀστοὶ τῆσδε βαρβάρου χθονός ,
sections.translation: All citizens of this barbarian land , hurl the reins on your horses , rush to the coast and seize what the Hellene ship

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: Ἑλληνίδος δέξεσθε , σὺν δὲ τῇ θεῷ
sections.translation: casts forth ! With the goddess ’ help , be eager to hunt down these impious men ! Drag the swift ships to the sea ! So that by sea and with pursuit on horseback by land , you may take them ; and hurl their bodies from the hard rock ,

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: ῥίψωμεν , ἢ σκόλοψι πήξωμεν δέμας .
sections.translation: or impale them on the stake .

sections.label: Section 1431
sections.original: ὑμᾶς δὲ τὰς τῶνδʼ ἴστορας βουλευμάτων ,
sections.translation: ( Turning to the Chorus .) As for you women , who knew about these plots , I will punish you later , when I am at leisure . But now in this present urgency , I will not remain still .

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ποῖ ποῖ διωγμὸν τόνδε πορθμεύεις , ἄναξ
sections.translation: Where , where are you carrying this pursuit , lord Thoas ? Listen the words of Athena , who is here . Cease to follow or to send an army pouring forth ; for Orestes came here , destined by the oracles of Loxias , to flee from the anger of the Furies ,

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: φεύγων ἀδελφῆς τʼ Ἄργος ἐσπέμψων δέμας
sections.translation: and to bring his sister to Argos and take the holy statue to my land , thus gaining a release from his present miseries . Thoas , I am speaking to you : you expect to take Orestes in the sea-swell and kill him ; but Poseidon , for my sake ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: πόντου τίθησι νῶτα πορθμεύειν πλάτῃ .
sections.translation: now lets him sail over the back of the waveless sea .

sections.label: Section 1446
sections.original: μαθὼν δʼ , Ὀρέστα , τὰς ἐμὰς ἐπιστολάς —
sections.translation: And you , Orestes , attend to my commands , for you hear the goddess ’ voice even though not present : go away with the statue and your sister ; and when you come to Athens , built by the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: χῶρός τις ἔστιν Ἀτθίδος πρὸς ἐσχάτοις
sections.translation: there is a place on the farthest borders of the Attic land , neighbor to the ridge of Carystia , sacred , and my people call it Halae . There build a temple and set up the image in it ; it will have its name from the Tauric land and from your labors ,

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: οὓς ἐξεμόχθεις περιπολῶν καθʼ Ἑλλάδα
sections.translation: which you have endured , wandering through Hellas and goaded by the Furies . And mortals will in future times celebrate Artemis Tauropolos with hymns . And establish this law : whenever the people keep the festival , let a sword be held

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: δέρῃ πρὸς ἀνδρὸς αἷμά τʼ ἐξανιέτω ,
sections.translation: to a man ’ s throat and draw out blood , in atonement for your sacrifice , so that the goddess may have her honors , and holiness is revered .

sections.label: Section 1462
sections.original: σὲ δʼ ἀμφὶ σεμνάς , Ἰφιγένεια , κλίμακας
sections.translation: You , Iphigenia , must be key-holder for this goddess on the hallowed stairs of Brauron , and will die there and be buried ;

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: ἄγαλμά σοι θήσουσιν εὐπήνους ὑφάς ,
sections.translation: and they will dedicate adornment to you , finely-woven robes which women who have died in childbirth leave in their homes . I charge you to send these Hellene women to their country , for their correct intentions . . . . For I saved you

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: καὶ πρίν σʼ Ἀρείοις ἐν πάγοις ψήφους ἴσας
sections.translation: before also , Orestes , on Ares ’ hill when the votes were equal ; and this will be the custom , for the one with equal votes to win . But , son of Agamemnon , take your sister away from this land . And you , Thoas , do not be angry .

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: ἄνασσʼ Ἀθάνα , τοῖσι τῶν θεῶν λόγοις
sections.translation: Lady Athena , whoever hears the words of the gods and does not obey , is not thinking rightly . I am not angry at Orestes , for going off with the goddess ’ image , or at his sister ; for what good is it to contend against the strength of gods ?

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: ἴτωσαν ἐς σὴν σὺν θεᾶς ἀγάλματι
sections.translation: Let them go to your land with the statue of the goddess , and let them establish it there , with good fortune . I will send these women also to fortunate Hellas , as you bid me . And I will stop the army

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: ναῶν τʼ ἐρετμά , σοὶ τάδʼ ὡς δοκεῖ , θεά .
sections.translation: and the ships I raised against the strangers , as you think this right , goddess .

sections.label: Section 1486
sections.original: αἰνῶ · τὸ γὰρ χρεὼν σοῦ τε καὶ θεῶν κρατεῖ .
sections.translation: I commend you ; for necessity rules both you and the gods .

sections.label: Section 1487
sections.original: ἴτʼ , ὦ πνοαί , ναυσθλοῦσθε τὸν Ἀγαμέμνονος
sections.translation: Go , winds , carry the son of Agamemnon to Athens by sea ; I will journey with them , and keep safe the holy image of my sister .
